page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.large is a general-purpose virtual server built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an ARMv8-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and is bundled with a 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1532.58 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1409.0. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core performance of 3042.87 and Geekbench multi-core score of 2532.0 are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 50.04 GB/sec but a weak write speed of 20.09 GB/sec. Complex tasks like ray tracing and image processing score in the poor tier, while single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average level of 24.13 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring fast temporary storage without the overhead of network-attached volumes. However, the 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and weak multi-threaded performance present clear tradeoffs for highly parallelized or network-heavy tasks. This server is best aligned with light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded applications, and small-scale database operations that benefit from local instance storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.large is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and powered by a 2.6 GHz ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of memory, and a bundled 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Benchmark evaluations show average single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, though multi-core performance and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. The server lacks GPU acceleration and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Qualitatively, the integrated local NVMe storage offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring rapid local read/write access without network storage overhead. It is suitable for light general-purpose computing, single-threaded applications, and small database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.large is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD, offering average single-core performance for light workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.large is an ARM64 server with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, suited for light general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton processing and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for balanced, entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 processor running at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers average single-core CPU performance but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and runs on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for light general-purpose computing, text processing, and small databases
